_description: Claude-consumed JSON registry for the naming pillar. Loaded by pre-write-guard.sh at runtime. Carries R-04, R-10, R-20, R-27, R-28.

_design_notes.evolution_protocol: R-37 atomic lockstep — naming-rules.json + Vault Architecture - Naming.md + pre-write-guard.sh + CLAUDE.md updated in one commit

rules.failure_mode: File written to undocumented vault-root drifts structure; Vault Architecture.md falls out of sync; navigation guides stale. Tier 3 advisory: write proceeds; operator told to move file or update Vault Architecture.md.
rules.rule_text: Files written to a vault-root directory must reside in a documented root. Known roots are a hardcoded allowlist in pre-write-guard.sh covering foundation-shipped system folders only (per canonical §F); user-created top-level folders register via overlay-master.frontmatter.path_routing extensions per canonical §H. A small set of top-level files is exempt from the root-must-be-a-directory rule (canonical §C mandatory files + System Backlog satellite). Adding a new foundation-shipped root requires R-10's checklist + R-37 lockstep — not mid-session improvisation.
rules._sp13_session_4_phase_1b_provenance: SP13 Session 4 Phase 1b (2026-05-14): known_roots reduced 15 → 8 per canonical §F (foundation-shipped system folders only: Archive/Daily/Inbox/Logs/Meetings/Plans/Skills/Vault Architecture). REMOVED user-named clusters (About Me / Artefact-BD / Engagements / Personal Initiatives / Reference / Tags / Dashboard) — user-named clusters belong in overlay-master.frontmatter.path_routing per canonical §H. About Me + Tags also retired per canonical §G (About Me/ folder retired entirely; Tags/ is Obsidian Make.md plugin artifact, not foundation-shipped). vault_root_file_exemptions: dropped Tasks.md per canonical §G (foundation-imposing a single task-tracking shape is unwarranted). System Backlog - Archive.md retained (consequence-of-lifecycle satellite; operator-disposition deferred). Closes C1 audit H6 + H7.

rules.source: ENFORCEMENT-MAP.md R-10 + vault CLAUDE.md New Structure Checklist + ../research/vault-construction/file-naming-conventions.md
rules.failure_mode: New top-level directories created mid-session without schema/hook/librarian/Vault-Architecture entries. Produces orphan roots invisible to walkers. Source: production-scale folder-drift incident (~20 orphan folders cleaned in a single vault cleanup pass).
rules.rule_text: Adding a new vault-root directory requires walking the 7-item New Structure Checklist deliberately. The checklist binds CLAUDE.md, vault-schema, hooks, and librarian into R-37 atomic lockstep so a new root cannot land in isolation.
checklist_items: Declare purpose — what archetype/content the directory holds
Declare consumer — which skill, hook, capability, or query parses this path
Update Vault Architecture.md (or the relevant pillar spoke)
Extend pre-write-guard.sh known-root list (R-04 allowlist update)
Commit R-37 atomic lockstep — all four surfaces move in one commit

enforcement_layer: promotion-pending: pre-commit hook if drift recurs
rules.failure_mode: Silent gitignore bypass — bare-name patterns match only at repo root; deeper instances slip through. A production incident saw ~100 files leak through bare-name gitignore in an initial commit of the reference deployment.
rules.rule_text: Gitignore patterns matching directory names at any depth must use the `**/` prefix. Bare-name patterns match only at repo root; `**/<name>/` matches anywhere in the tree. Documentary-only; promote to pre-commit hook if drift recurs.

rules.source: ENFORCEMENT-MAP.md R-27 + pre-write-guard.sh plan-creation block + vault root CLAUDE.md §Plan Creation Conventions
rules.failure_mode: Plans without numeric prefix or status break librarian plan-index regeneration. Shame slugs (auto-generated adjective-verb-noun) degrade plan-index, backlog routing, and search-by-name. A production audit found ~60% of plans in Unknown status before R-27 landed.
rules.rule_text: New or edited plan-root files must carry (a) an `NN-` numeric prefix on their top-level path segment AND (b) a status marker (`**Status:**` header bullet, YAML `status:` frontmatter, or `manifest.json` top-level `status` field). Sub-task files, handoffs, and orchestrator artifacts inherit from parent per R-28 and are NOT required to carry status. Tier 2 DENY at write-time.

rules.source: ENFORCEMENT-MAP.md R-28 + vault root CLAUDE.md §Plan Creation Conventions
rules.failure_mode: Sub-plan files at depth ≥ 3 without `parent_plan:` frontmatter are invisible to plan-tree walkers — librarian cannot resolve ancestry. Librarian-audit only; drift surfaced as findings, not write-time block. Pre-formalization, ~60 sub-plan files used the convention informally without resolver.
rules.rule_text: Sub-task files at depth ≥ 3 under `{plans_root}/` must carry `parent_plan: <top-level-slug>` frontmatter. Value is the top-level plan slug (no path, no extension). Plan-root files at depth 2, handoff.md at any depth, files under tests/, and files under _orchestrator/ are EXEMPT. Tier 1 advisory — librarian-audit only; conditional escalation if drift recurs.
rules.field_value_format: top-level plan slug (no path, no extension)
exemptions: Plan-root files at depth 2 (spec.md, tasks.md, handoff.md, 00-ideation-brief.md, README.md, manifest.json) — they ARE the parent
handoff.md at any depth (append-only session record)
Files under tests/ (synthetic fixtures, not plan state)
Files under _orchestrator/ (orchestrator runtime, not plan state)
rules.escalation_posture: Librarian-audit only matching the shame-slug enforcement precedent. Escalate to pre-write-guard only if write-time drift recurs despite sanctioned creation paths.
